report_subtitle: River-first contextual EDA with Hartwell, Russell, and Thurmond as operational annexes
context_summary: Start from the Savannah River mainstem, then add river pressures and pollutants, then use Hartwell, Russell, and Thurmond as explanatory support before bridging to the Thurmond sediment interpretation.
narrative_rules: Keep the Savannah River as the protagonist of the HTML.
Treat pressures and pollutants on the river as analytical core.
Use Hartwell, Russell, and Thurmond as explanatory support only.
Keep the 20-year target visible and separate from the real returned coverage.
Do not imply three-reservoir parity unless the EDA delivers it.

coverage_summary.rule: Always request a 20-year historical window when the source allows it, and separate the returned coverage from the target.

source_note.text: Metrics come from local staging tables, figures come from local EDA exports, and narrative text is synthesized only from structured context with traceable file paths.

sections.title: River signal first
sections.body: Lead with Savannah River hydrology and continuity before discussing reservoir behavior.

sections.title: River pressures and pollutants
sections.body: Make pollutants, pressures, impairments, dischargers, turbidity or sediment proxies, and watershed stressors visible in the river narrative.

sections.title: Coverage status against the 20-year target
sections.body: Expose the real returned period by source, layer, and entity whenever it falls short of the 20-year target.

sections.title: Reservoir modulation as explanatory annex
sections.body: Use Hartwell, Russell, and Thurmond to explain the river signal, not to replace it.

sections.title: Bridge to the Thurmond sediment interpretation
sections.body: Only after the river and pressure context is established should the HTML bridge toward the Thurmond sediment evidence.

sections.title: Methods, provenance, and remaining gaps
sections.body: Keep artifact paths, provenance, and unresolved gaps visible.

figures.title: Mainstem hydrologic coverage at the long-horizon USGS sites
Reservoir operations and forebay WQP as explanatory annex
Bridge from river signal to the Thurmond sediment interpretation
